evals.prompt: Build me a WBS for our ServiceNow ticket migration. I'm the PM (Ken Mulford, sponsor Priya Patel). Three big pieces: (1) ticket migration itself — Marcus Chen's team, probably a month total, and includes mapping ticket fields (~3 days, Marcus), writing ETL scripts (~1 week, one of Marcus's engineers TBD), and running a mock cutover (~2 days, Marcus). (2) Agent training — Taylor Kim running workshops, ~2 weeks total, includes writing a training deck (~3 days, Taylor), running 3 workshops (~1 day each, Taylor), and a knowledge check (~1 day, Taylor). (3) Salesforce and Slack integrations — owners TBD, no estimates yet. Target deadline October.
evals.expected_output: A WBS with 1 project, 3 deliverables (Ticket migration, Agent training, Integrations), and level-3 work packages under the first two. Ticket migration should have 3 WPs. Agent training should have 3 WPs (deck, workshops, knowledge check). Integrations should either have no children or [TBD] children — owners and effort must be TBD for integration WPs. Effort numbers should match user's words (e.g. 24h for 3 days, not invented). No fabricated 'Initiate/Plan/Close' phases. Raj Patel should not appear (Marcus's manager, reporting line only).

evals.prompt: I need a WBS for a finance dashboard project. I'm PM Alex Park. I know the big deliverables: data pipeline from our warehouse, the dashboard UI itself, user acceptance testing, and deployment. I don't have owners or estimates yet — we haven't staffed it.
evals.expected_output: A mostly-TBD WBS. Level 1 = Finance Dashboard project. Level 2 = four deliverables (Data pipeline, Dashboard UI, UAT, Deployment). Level 3 = all [TBD] or empty. Owners all [TBD]. Effort/duration all [TBD]. Draft Gaps sheet should list all the gaps. Should NOT invent work packages like 'Requirements gathering' or 'Data modeling' if user didn't name them. Should NOT invent owners or estimates.

evals.prompt: Project Aurora WBS. I'm Ken Mulford (PM), sponsor Mei Tanaka. Scope: new auth service replacing our middleware, plus migrating 40 apps. Aisha Khan leads backend (reports to Raj Patel). Noor Hassan owns security review (reports to Elena Volkov). Backend work: design threat model (Noor, 2 days), implement core auth service (Aisha, ~2 weeks), write migration SDK (Aisha, ~1 week), load test (Aisha, 3 days). Migration work: app owner onboarding (owner TBD, ~1 week), app migration waves (owner TBD — we're doing 4 waves of 10 apps each, ~1 week per wave). Compliance review by Q2.
evals.expected_output: A WBS with 1 project (Project Aurora), 2 deliverables (Backend, Migration). Backend has 4 work packages: threat model (Noor, 16h), core auth (Aisha, ~80h or [TBD — exceeds 80hr rule]), migration SDK (Aisha, ~40h), load test (Aisha, 24h). Migration has 2 work packages: onboarding [TBD owner], migration waves [TBD owner]. Raj Patel and Elena Volkov must NOT appear as owners — reporting lines only. Integrity sheet should flag the core auth work package if it was estimated at >80h.
